data.title: Memcyco secures $37M in Series A funding to combat digital impersonation and account takeover fraud, total funding now at $47M.
data.description: In Boston, Memcyco, a digital risk protection platform, has secured $37 million in Series A funding, totaling $47 million in funding. The oversubscribed round was led by new investors NAventures, E. León Jimenes, and PagsGroup, Steve Pagliuca's family office, with participation from existing investors. The platform focuses on protecting enterprises from brand impersonation scams and account takeover (ATO) in real-time.

data.title: Jan 2026 Procurement Magazine highlights SAP, Sarantis, and Equans on AI, strategy, and sustainable sourcing trends.
data.description: The January 2026 issue of Procurement Magazine showcases the AI strategies of SAP, Sarantis Group, and Equans, focusing on sustainable sourcing and the evolution of procurement leadership. The issue delves into the innovative approaches these companies are taking to incorporate artificial intelligence into their procurement processes. Key themes explored include utilizing AI to enhance sustainability efforts in sourcing practices and the shifting landscape of leadership in the procurement sector. Through in-depth interviews and analysis, the magazine highlights the commitment of these industry leaders to driving efficiency, sustainability, and strategic growth through AI implementation.

data.title: Gold mining stocks have outperformed S&P 500 by 10x, with VanEck Gold Miners ETF (GDX) climbing 187% in 12 months. Investors ponder if the rally will continue.
data.description: Gold mining stocks have seen an explosive rally in global markets, outperforming U.S. equities by a significant margin. Gold surged above $5,000 per ounce, silver went past $110, and mining equities continued to climb since mid-2025. The VanEck Gold Miners ETF (NYSE: GDX) has risen by 187% in the past 12 months, while the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSE: SPY) only returned about 16%. This massive gap indicates gold miners are on track to outperform the broader market by more than 10 times. Investors are now questioning if the gold miners sector has reached its peak or if the rally is still in its early stages. The sector's strong performance is backed by fundamentals, with gold having its best year since 1978 and silver since 1979 in 2025. Gold mining stocks appear to be on an upward trajectory, but uncertainty remains regarding their future potential.

data.title: CRISPR Therapeutics AG (NASDAQ: CRSP) faces pressure with insider sales, slow revenue growth; favored by Cathie Wood's Ark Invest.
data.description: A Swiss-American biotechnology company, CRISPR Therapeutics AG (NASDAQ: CRSP), is facing challenges with insider sales, slow revenue growth, and low adoption of its main products. Despite being prominent in funds managed by Cathie Wood's Ark Invest, like Ark Innovation ETF (BATS: ARKK) and Ark Genomic Revolution ETF (BATS: ARKG), the company's Momentum score has been declining in recent weeks. The company's stock performance is a concern, as highlighted in Benzinga's Edge Stock Rankings. For more details, the complete story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Government shutdown odds have increased from 10% to 79%, with prediction markets confirming Jeremy Siegel's warning of a sudden closure threat. Markets had previously priced the risk as negligible, but sentiment shifted dramatically, with Polymarket and Kalshi data showing shutdown probabilities at 79% and nearly 77% respectively. Siegel warns of market volatility ahead.
data.description: Wharton Professor Jeremy Siegel warns of a heightened risk of a government shutdown in Washington, as Democrats disrupt budget negotiations, leading prediction markets to increase the chance from less than 10% to over 70%. Data from Polymarket and Kalshi show shutdown odds at 79% and nearly 77%, respectively, just days before the deadline. Siegel believes this volatility could hinder market growth in the short term. The sudden disruption has caused significant reevaluation of the situation, impacting market sentiment and potentially leading to adverse market implications.
Musk's SpaceX is in merger talks with xAI ahead of a planned IPO this year. xai shares would be exchanged for SpaceX shares under the deal - source

data.title: ReelTime Media reduces outstanding debt by half, total debt reduced by over 64% in the past year, focusing on balance-sheet transformation.
data.description: Nvidia, Oracle, AMD, Alphabet, and Microsoft are increasing their investment in capital-intensive AI infrastructure, leveraging funds for expansion. In contrast, ReelTime is focused on disciplined debt reduction and transforming its balance sheet. This strategic move by Nvidia, Oracle, AMD, Alphabet, and Microsoft comes as they aim to boost their presence in the AI sector. On the other hand, ReelTime's emphasis on reducing debt and transforming its financial structure sets it apart from its counterparts.

data.title: Eckoh partners with boost.ai for innovative Conversational AI in secure customer engagement, PCI DSS compliant.
data.description: A partnership has been formed between Eckoh and boost.ai in Hertfordshire, England, both renowned for their expertise in secure customer engagement. This collaboration signifies a significant advancement for the companies and the industries they cater to. It involves the integration of robust generative AI agents with the high level of security provided by a platform accredited with level 1 PCI DSS and SOC2 standards. This merger aims to revolutionize the customer experience in regulated sectors with cutting-edge technology.
GDP is strong, but jobs are not. Fed models may turn hawkish on growth alone, yet policy decisions are likely to lean more on employment than GDP.

data.title: C.H. Robinson beats expectations, reaching record high despite industry challenges with global freight demand and rising trucking spot costs.
data.description: Investors found relief in the latest results from C.H. Robinson, released late on Wednesday. The company's ability to defy industry trends amid weak global freight demand and increasing trucking spot costs was especially noteworthy. As a result, the logistics firm's stock climbed to a record high, indicating positive performance.

data.title: Liberty Hill Foundation expands executive team to boost organization's impact after 50 years.Characters: 86
data.description: Liberty Hill Foundation, marking its 50th anniversary, revealed a significant development in Los Angeles on Jan. 29, 2026. The foundation disclosed the augmentation of its executive leadership squad to boost its operational framework. This step aims to further bolster the foundation's objective of backing grassroots organizing initiatives in LA, aligning with its longstanding mission.

data.title: Longevity biotech stocks see surge as $27 trillion healthcare shift favors cell restoration by 2030.
data.description: Avant Technologies Inc., based in Vancouver, BC, issued a news release on Jan. 29, 2026, stating that global capital is shifting towards "Longevity Infrastructure," which is anticipated to reach a value of $27 trillion by 2030. This significant transition is impacting various sectors and industries.

data.title: Saudi Arabia defense industry expenditure to reach $72.5 billion by 2025, with 7.7% CAGR; research report details market trends.
data.description: A report titled "Saudi Arabia Defense Market - Size And Trends, Budget Allocation, Regulations, Key Acquisitions, Competitive Landscape And Forecast, 2025-2030" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The report presents a forecast of the Saudi Arabia defense market's size and growth rate for the upcoming five years. It includes an analysis of the industry, highlighting market drivers, upcoming technology trends, and significant challenges encountered by market players. This report aims to provide insights into the market landscape.

data.title: OPSWAT names Bill Carey as CFO to oversee global growth, leveraging his tech finance expertise. TAMPA, Jan. 29, 2026.
data.description: A technology finance executive, possessing experience with IPOs and public entities, has been appointed to oversee the next phase of global growth for a cybersecurity company based in TAMPA, Fla. On January 29, 2026, OPSWAT, known for its critical infrastructure protection (CIP) cybersecurity solutions, revealed the update.

data.title: TechCreate issued a statement in response to recent trading activity, confirm no undisclosed material information. NYSE:TGCL.
data.description: TechCreate Group Ltd. (NYSE American: TCGL), a technology consultancy and software solutions provider focusing on payment solutions, cybersecurity, and digital services, responded to NYSE American's inquiry about recent trading in its ordinary shares. The company confirmed it does not possess undisclosed material nonpublic information that could explain the activity.

data.title: Reading Horizons Elevate Essentials wins Tech & Learning's Award of Excellence for Secondary Education. Unique program for literacy intervention.
data.description: In Salt Lake City, a renowned provider of structured literacy, Reading Horizons®, revealed their victory in the Tech & Learning Awards of Excellence: Best of 2025 Secondary Education section. The honored program, Reading Horizons Elevate Essentials, tackles a crucial need in secondary literacy intervention. It combines over 40 years of experience in phonics and decoding instruction, offering explicit, systematic, and sequential methods. This recognition highlights the program’s effectiveness in addressing secondary literacy challenges.

data.title: FOX Nation premieres "The White House" docudrama on Feb 6, exploring early presidents, with 2 new episodes.
data.description: A special program on FOX Nation will offer viewers a glimpse into the intimate lives of America's past leaders as the country nears its 250th anniversary. The series will premiere on February 6, featuring a total of two new episodes. Viewers will have the opportunity to explore the historical residences where key figures lived, made strategic decisions, and battled for the young nation's survival.

data.title: Tom Lee advises not to abandon Ethereum as it plunges 6% while gold and silver surge 18% and 52% YTD, creating a vortex diverting investor attention from crypto and other themes. Lee urges to own what works and not fall for FOMO.
data.description: Tom Lee from Fundstrat advised investors against giving in to FOMO and selling off Ethereum (ETH) despite its 6% drop on Thursday. This decline coincided with an ongoing surge in gold and silver prices, which have increased by 18% and 52% year-to-date respectively. BitMine (BMNR) pointed out this "astounding and impressive" trend, labeling it as the "Gold Vortex Problem". The company warned that this rally is drawing attention away from promising sectors like AI and cryptocurrency. Lee emphasized the importance of sticking with successful investments, cautioning against letting FOMO sway decisions. The issue of capital rotation was also linked to Lee's previous analysis, highlighting how the metals rally is diverting capital from the crypto market as traders allocate funds differently.
